checked.comment: 403 to WebFetch agent but 200 with full content via browser UA. Both claimed quotes found verbatim: colocation connects directly to arrowhead+J-GATE (東証及びOSEのシステム...に直接接続); Kansai Proximity = designated DC operator adjacent to AP4 (AP4に隣接している指定データセンター事業者).

J-GATE3.0 go-live release. Exact quote present, correctly attributed: comment by OSE President & CEO Moriyuki Iwanaga (大阪取引所代表取締役社長 岩永守幸) stating the JPX backup center had until then been in Kanto; also confirms Tokyo-Osaka twin-hub systems monitoring.

WebFetch blocks web.archive.org; downloaded via curl (200), valid 2019 Colt PDF, read in full. Exact quote verified: 'Premium colocation at the @Tokyo CC2 data centre on the same floor as the Japan Exchange Group (JPX) matching engine'. US-based prop trading firm, ~1M trades/day, 10Gbps arrownet cross-connect.
Original colt.net URL returns 404 as the researcher stated — the Wayback URL is the correct citation.
J-GATE system overview: 注文処理レイテンシ（50％タイル）40マイクロ秒 and 100,000件／秒 both present — capacity note figures verified.

週刊BCN 2011-09-21 article (Phillip Securities Singapore / KVH). Exact quote present: KVH proximity service based on the KVH Tokyo data center near major domestic exchanges, selling low-latency access to OSE J-GATE — derivatives proximity was Tokyo-based from 2011.

Title: 首都直下地震等に備えた関西データセンター等の整備について. Exact quote present; construction table verified: derivatives 2021-09-21, cash equities 2022-07-04, listed-derivatives clearing 2022-07-19 — all dates in the capacity note check out.
JPX Service Desk FAQ titled '回線のアクセスポイント（AP）の場所を教えてください'. Lists AP2: さいたま, AP3: 豊洲, AP4: 大阪 — AP4=Osaka (city-level) and AP3=Toyosu both verified.

Kansai Proximity Service live commercial offering; exact arrownet-line quote present. Also carries ラック当たり8KVAまで, sourcing the 8kVA rack spec in record 1's capacity note. Could not verify the '(updated 2025-12-18)' date in the publisher string — dropped.

arrownet page: WDM 10Gbps 完全異経路の安全な光ファイバーリング網 connecting access points and the secondary center; colo one-way ~3µs. Verifies arc operator (JPX) + medium (fiber); Osaka endpoint comes from faq/5137 and the Kansai DC release, both verified.
Added corroboration: PeeringDB facility AT TOKYO (CC1/CC2), Tokyo Koto-ku, coordinates 35.645435/139.793285 — grounds the approximate pin for record 1.

records.site_name: JPX Primary Data Center (arrowhead + J-GATE matching & colocation), @Tokyo CC2
records.operator_or_venue: Japan Exchange Group (TSE arrowhead + OSE/TOCOM J-GATE); facility identified as AT TOKYO @Tokyo CC2
records.firms_linked: JPX co-location participants (HFT market makers, prop firms); unnamed US proprietary trading firm via Colt managed colocation
records.coord_basis: JPX does not publish the primary DC address. Colt case study (archived, verified) names @Tokyo CC2; PeeringDB facility record fac/738 places AT TOKYO CC1/CC2 in Koto-ku at 35.6454,139.7933 (used here); JPX Service Desk FAQ 5137 places arrownet AP3 at Toyosu, Koto-ku. Not a JPX-documented street address — remains approximate pending geocode per project rule 3.
records.capacity_note: J-GATE (Genium INET) order latency 40 microseconds median, ~100,000 orders/sec; colo racks up to 8kVA, colo-to-engine ~3 microseconds one-way (JPX arrownet page). Key fact: derivatives matching for 'Osaka' Exchange (Nikkei 225 futures/options) physically runs here alongside arrowhead — there is no Osaka execution site; TSE-OSE arbitrage is intra-data-center.

evidence.quote: コロケーションエリアからは東証及びOSEのシステム（arrowhead、J-GATE、ToSTNeT、相場報道システム）に直接接続することが可能
evidence.supports: JPX colocation is at the JPX Primary site and connects directly to both arrowhead and J-GATE — cash and derivatives matching co-located at one site.

evidence.quote: これまで関東に設置されていたJPXグループのバックアップセンターを関西に移転した第一弾のシステム
evidence.supports: OSE President & CEO Moriyuki Iwanaga states JPX group's backup center had until 2021 been in Kanto — J-GATE primary and backup were both Tokyo-region; nothing matched in Osaka.

evidence.publisher: Colt Technology Services (2019 case study, archived)
evidence.quote: Premium colocation at the @Tokyo CC2 data centre on the same floor as the Japan Exchange Group (JPX) matching engine
evidence.supports: Publicly names @Tokyo CC2 as the facility housing the JPX matching engine; original colt.net URL 404s (checked), Wayback copy fetched and read in full.
Independent public network-forensics record placing AT TOKYO CC1/CC2 in Koto-ku with coordinates; basis for the approximate pin.

evidence.quote: 注文処理レイテンシ（50％タイル） 40マイクロ秒
evidence.supports: J-GATE performance figures for capacity note (Genium INET, 100,000 orders/sec verified on same page).
Colocation rack power spec (up to 8kVA per rack) cited in capacity note.

evidence.quote: KVHの高速光ファイバー網と国内主要取引所に近接するKVH東京データセンターを基盤にしたサービス
evidence.supports: As early as 2011, low-latency proximity access to OSE J-GATE was sold from a Tokyo data center — the derivatives engine was Tokyo-region from launch.

records.confidence_rationale: All URLs re-fetched and quotes verified verbatim. J-GATE + colocation at the JPX Primary site (Tokyo region) is confirmed by JPX primary sources (FAQ, news releases, system pages). The @Tokyo CC2 identification rests on a Colt operator case study (archived copy verified; original 404s), corroborated by PeeringDB's Koto-ku facility record. Coordinates approximate — no JPX-published address exists.

records.site_name: JPX Kansai Data Center & Kansai Proximity Service (arrownet AP4), Osaka
records.operator_or_venue: Japan Exchange Group (Kansai backup DC; proximity area run by an unnamed designated data center operator adjacent to arrownet AP4)
records.firms_linked: JPX trading participants, information vendors and ISVs using Kansai Proximity racks; JPX/OSE systems operations teams (Tokyo-Osaka twin-hub monitoring)
records.coord_basis: JPX discloses only 'Kansai region' for the backup DC and lists arrownet AP4 simply as '大阪' (Osaka) in Service Desk FAQ 5137 (fetched); Osaka city centroid used. Exact facility undisclosed.
records.capacity_note: Backup matching site built against a Tokyo-inland-quake scenario: derivatives (J-GATE3.0) backup live 2021-09-21, cash equities backup 2022-07-04, listed-derivatives clearing 2022-07-19 (all 構築完了 per JPX 2023-01-20 table). Kansai Proximity Service (adjacent to AP4) sells rack space reaching the Tokyo matching engines over arrownet. Not a matching site in normal operation — trades still execute in Tokyo.

evidence.quote: 首都圏からの遠隔地である関西圏においてバックアップデータセンター（以下「関西DC」という。）の構築を進めている
evidence.supports: Kansai DC exists as JPX's remote backup; table dates each system's completion, incl. デリバティブ売買システム 2021年9月21日【構築完了】.

evidence.supports: Kansai Proximity Service = participant equipment hosted adjacent to arrownet AP4, with JPX-provided network to the trading systems.

evidence.supports: Official JPX statement that arrownet access point AP4 is in Osaka (city-level only).

evidence.supports: Kansai Proximity Service is a live commercial offering alongside Tokyo colocation.

records.confidence_rationale: All four evidence URLs re-fetched; every quote located verbatim in JPX primary sources (news release, official FAQ, Service Desk FAQ, connectivity page). Location honestly graded city_level because JPX discloses only 'Kansai'/'Osaka' and the facility operator is unnamed.

arcs.dest: JPX Kansai Data Center & Kansai Proximity Service (arrownet AP4), Osaka
arcs.note: Exchange-operated arrownet: WDM 10Gbps fully route-diverse fiber ring (完全異経路の安全な光ファイバーリング網, verified on page) linking the Tokyo primary center with access points and the secondary center; AP4 = Osaka per JPX Service Desk FAQ 5137 and the Kansai DC per JPX 2023-01-20 release (both fetched). Carries Kansai Proximity order flow to the Tokyo matching engines. Only documented Tokyo-Osaka low-latency trading link; no Tokyo-Osaka HFT microwave corridor found in the public record.

reasoning: Adversarial re-verification of all 10 cited URLs plus 2 added checks. JPX pages 403 the WebFetch agent but serve full content to a browser user-agent via curl (all 200); the archived Colt PDF was downloaded from the Wayback URL and read in full. Every claimed quote was found verbatim: JPX FAQ (colocation connects directly to arrowhead AND J-GATE at the primary site; Kansai Proximity adjacent to AP4), the 2021-09-21 J-GATE3.0 release (backup center 'until now in Kanto' — correctly attributed to OSE President Iwanaga), Service Desk FAQ 5137 (AP2 Saitama / AP3 Toyosu / AP4 Osaka), the 2023-01-20 Kansai DC release with the exact completion-date table (derivatives 2021-09-21, equities 2022-07-04, listed-derivatives clearing 2022-07-19), the J-GATE 40µs/100k-orders page, the connectivity page (Kansai Proximity arrownet lines; also sources the 8kVA rack spec), the arrownet page (WDM 10Gbps route-diverse fiber ring, colo 3µs one-way), and the 2019 Colt case study naming @Tokyo CC2 on the same floor as the JPX matching engine (original colt.net URL confirmed 404; archive copy is the right citation). The 2011 BCN/KVH article verifies J-GATE proximity was sold from a Tokyo DC at launch. Changes made: (1) record 1 pin refined to PeeringDB fac/738 coordinates (35.6454,139.7933, Koto-ku) with PeeringDB added as evidence — still coord_precision=approximate since JPX publishes no address; (2) connectivity page added to record 1 evidence to source the previously uncited 8kVA claim; (3) dropped an unverifiable '(updated 2025-12-18)' from a publisher string. Merge target T1-010 confirmed present in data/sites_seed.csv. Geography sane for both pins (Koto-ku Tokyo; Osaka centroid, city_level). An independent search found no Tokyo-Osaka HFT microwave link, consistent with the researcher's core (and essay-worthy) finding that 'Osaka' derivatives matching physically runs in Tokyo, so no microwave arc belongs on the map. Both records and the fiber arc survive at confirmed.
research_notes: The candidate's premise inverts on the evidence. (1) NO Tokyo-Osaka HFT microwave corridor exists in the public record: Japanese-language sweeps (超高速取引/マイクロ波/東阪間/無線 低遅延) plus McKay Brothers' own media page (mckay-brothers.com/media, fetched) and coverage page turn up Tokyo-Singapore (2016), Osaka-exchange DATA distributed via Tokyo CC2 to Singapore (2017), Tokyo-Busan, Tokyo-Shanghai/HK, transpacific (2022, with Go West Dec 2025) and Tokyo-Europe crypto routes — but no Tokyo-Osaka wireless service; the lead that McKay lists 'Tokyo-Osaka latencies between JPX colo sites' could not be substantiated (their /latencies page 404s; coverage lists Tokyo only for Japan). MIC's 無線局等情報検索 register is an interactive form not directly crawlable, and no press/academic reference to trading-linked Tokyo-Osaka fixed links was found; the only Tokyo-Osaka microwave in the record is NTT's 1962 public-telecom trunk. (2) Reason: 'Osaka' derivatives matching physically runs in the Tokyo region. Since J-GATE's Feb 2011 launch, proximity access was sold from KVH's Tokyo DC (週刊BCN 2011); since the July 2016 J-GATE replacement JPX has run integrated colocation at its Primary site connecting directly to BOTH arrowhead and J-GATE (JPX FAQ); the OSE CEO's 2021-09-21 statement confirms even the group backup center 'had until now been located in Kanto'. An archived 2019 Colt case study publicly names @Tokyo CC2 as the floor hosting the JPX matching engine. So TSE-OSE (cash vs Nikkei 225 futures) latency arbitrage is an intra-data-center race in Koto-ku, Tokyo — a strong essay point: the corridor consumed itself. (3) What IS mappable: JPX's Kansai backup DC (derivatives backup live 2021-09-21, equities 2022-07-04) and the new Kansai Proximity Service adjacent to arrownet AP4 (Osaka) — one new city-level execution pin plus a confirmed JPX-operated Tokyo-Osaka fiber arc (arrownet ring). Record 1 is an enrichment/merge for existing T1-010 (adds J-GATE + @Tokyo CC2 sourcing; suggest replacing its symbolic 35.68,139.76 with Koto-ku ~35.65,139.80, still approximate). Out-of-scope lead worth a separate candidate: McKay Brothers + Go West Chicago-Tokyo service announced Dec 2025 (mckay-brothers.com/media). Suggested new site_id: T1-0xx 'JPX-KANSAI-AP4'.
